title: Dry Bulk performance
summary:
Main
By Michalis Voutsinas
As the dry bulk sector progresses into the first weeks of 2026, the market is entering what is traditionally the softest period of the year, with seasonal patterns exerting their usual downward pressure on freight rates across all segments. Yet unlike previous cycles, Q1 has begun from a noticeably firmer base, reflecting the structural tightness carried over from 2025 and the continued firmness in major commodity flows. The usual early-year slowdown is therefore unfolding within a more balanced framework, with the market demonstrating resilience even as seasonal demand ebbs.
